title: hypericum tetrapterum var. anagallifolium
intro: Hypericum tetrapterum var. anagallifolium, also known as St. John's Wort, is a plant species within the Hypericum genus, characterized by its four-angled stems and flowers that bloom in the summer months. The medicinal properties of Hypericum tetrapterum var. anagallifolium include its high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, which have been found to have antidepressant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to treat mild to moderate depression, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Hypericum tetrapterum var. anagallifolium can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, which are often used to support mental and physical well-being.
